user_query:
I'm moderating a virology Q&A forum and I need to clean up our old analysis script so I can generate reliable summaries for digest posts. Please perform a focused code review on scripts/legacy_analysis.py and refactor it into a maintainable, testable script at src/analyze_forum.py that reads input/posts.csv and produces deterministic, structured outputs. Do not change any files under input/. Use only the provided CSV as input.

Refactoring and output requirements:
1) General
- Load records from input/posts.csv and normalize types: upvotes and replies as integers, accepted as a boolean, date parsed as YYYY-MM-DD.
- Implement clear functions with no global mutable state, basic input validation, and docstring at the top summarizing the key issues you found in legacy_analysis.py and how the refactor addresses them.
- Make the script idempotent: re-running it overwrites the same outputs.
- Create the outputs directory if it does not exist and write all deliverables there.

2) Deliverables (exact paths and structures)
- outputs/top_posts_2023_influenza.csv: Top 5 posts from 2023 that include the tag "Influenza". Rank by upvotes descending; tie-breakers: replies descending, then date ascending, then id ascending. Include a header and exactly these columns in this order: id,title,author,date,upvotes,replies,tags.
- outputs/author_ranking.csv: One row per author with columns: author,total_posts,total_upvotes,avg_upvotes,accepted_rate. Compute totals across all records. avg_upvotes = total_upvotes/total_posts rounded to 2 decimals. accepted_rate = accepted_true_count/total_posts rounded to 2 decimals. Sort by total_upvotes descending, then total_posts descending, then author ascending. Include a header.
- outputs/tag_summary.csv: One row per unique tag (split the semicolon-delimited tags field). Columns: tag,total_posts,total_upvotes,avg_upvotes,accepted_rate,posts_2024. avg_upvotes and accepted_rate rounded to 2 decimals. posts_2024 = count of posts with date in 2024 for that tag. Sort by total_upvotes descending, then tag ascending. Include a header.
- outputs/author_stats.json: A JSON array of objects where each object has: author,total_posts,total_upvotes,avg_upvotes,accepted_rate (same definitions as author_ranking.csv). The array should include all authors (not just top N).

3) Notes on correctness
- Filtering, sorting, and ranking must follow the rules above exactly to ensure deterministic results.
- All outputs must be derived solely from input/posts.csv.

Please keep legacy_analysis.py for reference, but do your refactor in src/analyze_forum.py and ensure running the new script produces the four files under outputs/ with the specified structures. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
